name: Tarte Tatin (Apples)
recipeIngredient: 10 tablespoons Unsalted butter, cut into small pieces
1/2 cup Unsalted butter, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
Pastry: In a large bowl, combine all-purpose flour and rice flour. With a pastry blender or 2 knives, cut in 10 tablespoons butter until the mixture resembles fine crumbs. Stir in superfine sugar. Add egg and mix well. Knead dough in the bowl until it clings together. Form dough into a disk shape. Wrap and refrigerate while preparing the topping.
Apple topping: Melt 1/2 cup butter in a heavy, oven-safe skillet with straight sides over medium-high heat. Sprinkle granulated sugar evenly over the melted butter. Do not stir. Remove from heat. Peel, core, and thinly slice apples. Arrange apple slices overlapping in 2 layers of concentric circles over the butter mixture in the skillet. Sprinkle with lemon zest.
Cook over medium-high heat until the sugar starts to caramelize and turns golden brown as it bubbles up around the sides of the pan. Remove from heat.
Assemble: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). On a lightly floured surface, roll out pastry about 1/4 inch thick into a circle the same size as the top inside edge of the skillet. Trim edges evenly. Fit pastry over apples in the skillet, pressing down lightly. Place skillet on a baking sheet. Bake at 350°F for 35 to 45 minutes until the pastry is nicely browned. Cool in pan set on a rack for 2 minutes. Invert onto a heat-proof serving plate and serve warm.
